code: @Manual{,   title = {FounderForge: Prepare Marker Data as AlphaSimR Founder Populations},   author = {Ebenezer Ogoe},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://ebenogoe.github.io/FounderForge/}, }

title: FounderForge
text: Prepare marker data founder population AlphaSimR simulations - coding required. FounderForge point--click Shiny application (built golem framework packaged R package) lets breeders, students researchers prepare real marker data founder population AlphaSimR simulations, without writing R.

title: FounderForge
text: Starting AlphaSimR breeding simulation real data normally means hand-writing script read genotypes, quality-control filter , split haplotypes, build genetic map, call newMapPop(). FounderForge wraps whole process guided wizard non-programmer can reliably, see happening step, get back exactly objects AlphaSimR needs.

title: FounderForge
text: Guided five-step wizard (upload → review → filter → traits → build & export). Multiple input formats automatic format detection validation. Quality-control dashboard: marker/individual counts, missingness, MAF, heterozygosity, per-chromosome distribution, plots. Flexible filtering: MAF, per-SNP per-individual missingness, monomorphic-marker removal, minimum SNPs per chromosome, dropping named individuals - live “markers retained” preview. Choice import strategy (outbred, inbred/doubled-haploid, importInbredGeno), explained plain language, heterozygosity handled correctly material. Optional additive-trait definition also produce AlphaSimR SimParam. Optional /PCA validation confirm import preserved population structure, downloadable plot. Organised, timestamped outputs plus full text log run, including explicit “assumptions approximations” section. Helpful, specific error messages; progress feedback every long-running step. Bundled sample datasets can try app immediately.

title: FounderForge
text: wide numeric/HapMap-style tables, FounderForge auto-detects chromosome, position marker-id columns (chrom, pos, rs#) ignores standard metadata columns (alleles, strand, REFERENCE_GENOME, etc.). Sample names suffixes LINE1:FLOWCELL:LANE can cleaned one click.

title: FounderForge
text: Upload - choose file (bundled sample), pick import strategy ploidy. detected file type validation summary shown, scrollable preview first rows columns. Review & QC - inspect counts, missingness, MAF per-chromosome marker distribution changing anything. Filter - apply MAF / missingness / monomorphic / chromosome-size filters preview many markers remain. Traits (optional) - add additive traits (QTL per chromosome, mean, variance, heritability) build SimParam. Build & export - choose output folder, build founder population, optionally run PCA check, save everything. hood: genotypes normalised 0/1/2 matrix, missing calls imputed, genotype split haplotypes (preserving heterozygosity outbred material), per-chromosome genetic map built (positions Morgans, chromosome starting 0), AlphaSimR::newMapPop() (importInbredGeno()) called.

title: FounderForge
text: full wizard, welcome finished founder population (sped ): Upload validate data, live preview detected file type: Quality-control review filtering (counts, MAF, missingness, markers per chromosome): Build founder population confirm import /PCA validation:

what: Outputs and how they fit into AlphaSimR
title: FounderForge
text: run writes timestamped folder: genmap.rds haplotypes.rds precisely inputs AlphaSimR::newMapPop(); founder_pop.rds ready--use MapPop. start simulation saved run: rebuild_snippet.R reproduces founder population genmap.rds + haplotypes.rds, import fully auditable.
code: FounderForge_run_<timestamp>/ ├── founder_population/ │   ├── genmap.rds          # list of per-chromosome genetic maps (Morgans) │   ├── haplotypes.rds      # list of per-chromosome haplotype matrices │   ├── founder_pop.rds     # the AlphaSimR MapPop object │   ├── sim_param.rds       # AlphaSimR SimParam (only if traits were defined) │   └── rebuild_snippet.R   # the exact constructor call used, for reproducibility ├── qc/                     # qc_summary.csv and plots ├── validation/             # heterozygosity check, optional before/after PCA └── logs/                   # full run_log_<timestamp>.txt library(AlphaSimR)  founderPop <- readRDS("founder_population/founder_pop.rds") SP         <- readRDS("founder_population/sim_param.rds")  # if you defined traits # otherwise: SP <- SimParam$new(founderPop)  pop <- newPop(founderPop, simParam = SP) # ... now cross, select, phenotype, etc.

title: FounderForge
text: Requires R (>= 3.5.0). Install FounderForge dependencies: Key dependencies (installed automatically): AlphaSimR, vcfR, data.table, bslib, bsicons, shinyFiles, shinyWidgets, shinybusy, DT, ggplot2. patchwork suggested side--side PCA figure.

title: FounderForge
text: FounderForge designed run locally can read large genotype files directly disk (via shinyFiles) write outputs folder choose.

title: FounderForge
text: Bundled example datasets (300 markers x 30 individuals, 3 chromosomes) ship inst/extdata/ (sample_numeric.rds, sample_numeric.csv, sample.vcf, sample.hmp.txt). Load one click upload page, download Help & docs screen.

title: FounderForge
text: genetic map assumes uniform recombination rate (1 cM/Mb); supply real map accurate distances. outbred imports, phasing heterozygous sites arbitrary (genotypes split, phased). QTL positions placed random AlphaSimR; trait means, variances heritabilities user-supplied assumptions. recorded run’s log file.

title: FounderForge
text: Please open issue (email ebenezerogoe@gmail.com) attach logs/ file affected run - captures every step import export.

what: FounderForge: Prepare Marker Data as AlphaSimR Founder Populations — FounderForge-package
title: FounderForge: Prepare Marker Data as AlphaSimR Founder Populations — FounderForge-package
text: point--click Shiny application helps non-programmers prepare marker data (VCF, HapMap, numeric genotype matrices PLINK) founder population AlphaSimR simulations. guides users uploading data, quality-control review, filtering (MAF, missingness, monomorphic markers), optional trait definition export objects required AlphaSimR::newMapPop(), progress feedback, helpful errors, optional /PCA validation full text log run.

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
code: run_app(   onStart = NULL,   options = list(),   enableBookmarking = NULL,   uiPattern = "/",   ... )

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
text: onStart function called app actually run. needed shinyAppObj, since shinyAppDir case, global.R file can used purpose. options Named options passed runApp call (can following: "port", "launch.browser", "host", "quiet", "display.mode" "test.mode"). can also specify width height parameters provide hint embedding environment ideal height/width app. enableBookmarking Can one "url", "server", "disable". default value, NULL, respect setting previous calls  enableBookmarking(). See enableBookmarking() information bookmarking app. uiPattern regular expression applied GET request determine whether ui used handle request. Note entire request path must match regular expression order match considered successful. ... arguments pass golem_opts. See `?golem::get_golem_options` details.

title: FounderForge 0.1.0
text: First release. Point--click Shiny wizard prepare marker data (VCF, HapMap, numeric matrices, PLINK) AlphaSimR founder population. Quality-control review, flexible filtering (MAF, missingness, monomorphic markers, minimum SNPs per chromosome, individual dropping), optional additive trait definition, optional /PCA validation. Organised, timestamped outputs (genmap.rds, haplotypes.rds, founder_pop.rds, optional sim_param.rds, reproducible rebuild_snippet.R, QC/validation plots full run log).
